definition: A system of government by the whole population or all the eligible members of a state
example: Our constitution is called a democracy because power is in the hands not of a minority but of the whole people.

definition: The activities associated with the governance of a country or area
example: We do not say that a man who takes no interest in politics is a man who minds his own business; we say that he has no business here at all.

definition: The ability to do something that frightens one; bravery
example: The bravest are surely those who have the clearest vision of what is before them, glory and danger alike, and yet with courage go out to meet it.

definition: The process of removing impurities or unwanted elements from a substance
example: We cultivate refinement without extravagance and knowledge without effeminacy; wealth we employ more for use than for show.

definition: High respect; great esteem; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: It is only the love of honor that never grows old; and honor it is, not gain, as some would have it, that rejoices the heart of age and helplessness.

definition: An act of giving up something valued for the sake of something else regarded as more important
example: The sacrifice which they collectively made was individually repaid to them; for they received again each one for himself a praise which grows not old.

definition: Something handed down by a predecessor; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Legacy is the hinge of Pericles' Funeral Oration (Thucydides): without it the rest is decoration.

definition: The power or right to act, speak, or think as one wants
example: Make up your minds that happiness depends on being free, and freedom depends on being courageous.

definition: The state of being happy; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Make up your minds that happiness depends on being free, and freedom depends on being courageous.

definition: High renown or honor won by notable achievements; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: The bravest are surely those who have the clearest vision of what is before them, glory and danger alike, and yet notwithstanding, go out to meet it.

definition: Relating to a city or town, especially its administration; municipal
example: A civic argument runs through Pericles' Funeral Oration (Thucydides): conviction without cruelty, force without panic.

definition: Lack of restriction; accessibility; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Openness is the hinge of Pericles' Funeral Oration (Thucydides): without it the rest is decoration.

definition: The traditional male quality of being courageous and resilient
example: Manliness is the hinge of Pericles' Funeral Oration (Thucydides): without it the rest is decoration.

definition: The state of being prosperous; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Prosperity is the hinge of Pericles' Funeral Oration (Thucydides): without it the rest is decoration.

definition: A person or thing regarded in terms of their fitness to be imitated
example: We do not copy our neighbors, but are an example to them.

definition: Firm determination to do something; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Such is the Athens for which these men, in the assertion of their resolve not to lose her, nobly fought and died.

definition: Having or showing fine personal qualities or high moral principles
example: A noble argument runs through Pericles' Funeral Oration (Thucydides): conviction without cruelty, force without panic.

definition: The ability to think about or plan the future with imagination or wisdom
example: The bravest are surely those who have the clearest vision of what is before them, glory and danger alike, and yet notwithstanding, go out to meet it.

definition: The possibility of suffering harm or injury; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: The bravest are surely those who have the clearest vision of what is before them, glory and danger alike, and yet notwithstanding, go out to meet it.

definition: A large vault, typically an underground one, for burying the dead
example: The whole earth is the tomb of famous men; not only are they commemorated by columns and inscriptions in their own country, but in foreign lands there dwells also an unwritten memorial of them.
